scope_completeness.detail: All 37 material areas mapped to specific workflows with explicit evidence in descriptions. Coverage includes every material balance sheet line, both revenue streams, complex accounting areas (ASR/hedging/leases/taxes), all ICFR components, component/group audit across 40+ jurisdictions, first-year procedures, fraud procedures, specialist needs, all PCAOB communications, and filing/documentation requirements.

per_workflow_correctness.detail: All 33 leaf workflows' computed hours match description totals. Team compositions align with described work. One factual error found: opening_balance_procedures cited 'AS 2510' (Auditing Inventories) instead of 'AS 2101' (Audit Planning) for opening balance procedures in a first-year engagement. Corrected. All other PCAOB standard citations verified correct against pcaobus.org: AS 2801 (Subsequent Events), AS 2610 (Predecessor Communication), AS 2110 (Risk Assessment), AS 2401 (Fraud), AS 2201 (ICFR), AS 1220 (EQR), AS 1301/1305 (Communications), AS 3101 (Audit Report), AS 1215 (Documentation).

domain_plausibility.detail: All 33 leaf workflows pass domain plausibility. Hours decompose into concrete tasks matching computed values. AI capabilities described (full-population transaction testing, document analysis, report drafting) are feasible with current frontier models. Irreducible human judgment areas (fraud brainstorming, EQR, physical walkthroughs, partner interviews, opinion signing) have sufficient hours. Stream proportions reasonable: Control 47.9%, Substantive 34.4%, Reporting 8.9%, Specialist 4.8%, Scoping 2.5%, Setup 1.4%. Partner leverage (EP 8.2% of hours) appropriate for AI-first model. Cross-engagement reduction 97.8% (4,143h vs EY ~185,000h) driven by AI full-population testing with human oversight for exceptions and judgment.

scope_completeness.detail: All 36 material areas mapped to specific workflows with explicit evidence in descriptions. cost_reasonableness.detail: Total cost $13.3M for Fortune 50 integrated audit (well above $1M AI-first floor). Direct human labor $5.4M at 10x EY-equivalent rates. Overhead $7.9M at 145% covers technology, insurance, regulatory, admin, quality, facilities, travel, training for multi-entity startup with single mega-cap engagement. headline_soundness.detail: 4,143 human hours (vs EY ~185,000h = 97.8% reduction via AI). 44 headcount (vs EY ~500). $13.3M total cost (vs EY ~$30M+). 23-week timeline driven by external bottlenecks (predecessor, client data, books closing Sept 27, 60-day LAF filing deadline). AI-first economics: 44 humans vs. EY's ~450+ delivers 50x labor reduction while maintaining same PCAOB standards. Cost premium justified by 10x rates for elite specialists + 145% startup overhead. 23-week timeline matches external bottleneck constraints (predecessor response, client data availability, books closing September 27).

cost_reasonableness.detail: Total $13,304,826 for AI-first integrated audit of $416B Fortune 50 company. Breakdown: $5,423,276 direct human (40.8%), $7,265 direct AI (0.05%), $7,874,285 overhead at 145% (59.2%). Blended human rate: $1,309/hr. Per-stream: control_audit $5.75M (43% — largest, includes 7 sub-workflows and ITGC), substantive $4.11M (31% — 4 sub-workflows with full-population testing), reporting $1.88M (14% — EQR, opinion, statutory), specialist $0.70M (5% — tax-dominant), scoping $0.62M (5% — progressive three-pass), setup $0.25M (2% — predecessor transition). AI compute costs total $7,265 ($4,465 for analysis/reasoning tasks, $2,800 for population testing), representing <0.1% of total — AI value comes from labor hour reduction, not compute cost. Cost compares to estimated Big 4 fee of $25-30M at 190,000h: Superaudit achieves 97.8% hour reduction (4,143h) at ~50% cost reduction, justified by 10x premium rates ($3,200/hr partner vs Big 4 ~$320/hr) offset by AI-driven efficiency. Overhead at 145% is high but justified for startup with single mega-cap engagement (no amortization benefit), multi-jurisdiction regulatory costs, and heavy technology investment.
